name: history-workflow
description: Histories sample a single variable at a common interval during cycling. They are used for convergence checks, load/displacement curves, pore-pressure response, dynamic traces, and other validation outputs.

notes: All histories use one global sampling interval.
A smaller interval gives finer curves but increases stored data.

commands: zone history stress quantity yy position (0.5,0.5) label 'mid_sigy'
notes: FLAC2D positions use the (x,y) 2D tuple — NOT 'x y z'. FLAC3D uses (x,y,z).

Tensor attributes (stress, strain-rate, strain-increment) take a 'quantity' selector: xx/yy/zz/xy/xz/yz/maximum/minimum/intermediate/mean/norm/octahedral/shear-maximum/total-measure/volumetric/von-mises.
Use 'gp <gpid>' to track a gridpoint's value (this replaces the non-existent 'zone gridpoint history' command).
Use 'label <string>' to give the history a memorable name; the command still also assigns a numeric ID.
'history list all' prints ID / Name / Label / Position.

By default a new history's NAME equals its numeric ID as a string ('1','2',...). 'history rename' OVERWRITES the Name field — after renaming, the integer reference no longer matches and you must use the new string in 'history export'.
Rename uses 'history name <id-or-current-name> rename <new-string>' (binary-validated): the 'name' subcommand filters first, then 'rename' assigns.
The LABEL field is independent of Name — 'history export' resolves by NAME, not by Label.
'vs step' plots against step number; 'vs <other-history-id>' plots against another history.

management_keywords.description: Set the global sampling interval in cycles or steps.
List configured histories. Sub-keywords: all / labels / limits / series.
Erase stored history values while keeping history definitions.
Write selected histories to screen, file, or table. References histories by numeric ID (or by a name set via 'history rename').
Rename a history (give it a string name that 'history export' can resolve).
Filter subsequent 'history' subcommand by a history name pattern.
'history results active <bool>' — include history data in results export.

validation_notes: Name histories so exported curves remain self-describing.
For staged analysis, export or purge histories at stage boundaries when the baseline changes.
Use product-specific coordinates: avoid z-position probes in FLAC2D commands.
